reviews.review_msg: Daniel was a wonderful prof, super passionate about what he taught. Group project is a decent chunk of your grade, so make sure to spend time on that. Overall, course is pretty interesting and not TOO tough. Daniel though, he is great. Would take a class with him in a heart-beat.

Quizzes and assignments were quite difficult overall. Weekly quizzes are tricky - questions have multiple answers. Weekly assignments are difficult, but you get a partner - lecture notes don't really prepare you for them. Entire course has a ton of content, you'll fall behind really easily if you don't try hard to be on pace.
Enjoyable class but more should have been taught, I think. It didn't feel like it was taught at the 400 level.
Love Daniel. Super passionate, accessible, and helpful. The material itself is also pretty practical and useful.

Daniel is awesome. He's quite passionate about the courses he teaches and super approachable as a instructor. Cares about his students! His background is in statistics so I really recommend him for courses like BUS 464, 345, 445. His tests are usually based on his slides, so make sure you know them well and refer to the textbook as you need.

Daniel is brilliant, funny and great at teaching . He is extremely passionate about all things IT and is almost always available to help out. Do your readings, come prepared for class and ask him questions - he will have an answer for you! He is definitely one of my favourite professors at SFU and I would 100% take another class with him.

Loved this class! Teaches you how to create a database, website, and connect everything together into a working business application. Focus on website content rather than style (CSS), and very helpful with debugging. Places larger value on proper coding rather than the user interface but you can take a design class for that.

He's great! Very knowledgeable and engaging. His class is one of the few classes at Beedie, that I would say really have important real-world application, and not just concepts and keywords to throw out there.  He's also super helpful in tutorials, and really cares about the students. Very organized. A bit goofy, and endearing.

It does not seem like a 400 level course, more like a continuation of 237. Final paper, but no final. Very little amount of readings and work. Not much is learned. Most lectures are taken up by weekly presentations with topics and discussion that may or may not interest you. Marks fairly according to criteria although he takes his time.
